name: Ransomware Attack - Municipal Network
description: A ransomware variant encrypts file servers and spreads laterally across the municipal network via SMB. Active Directory is compromised. Email and ERP are inaccessible.

impact.data_at_risk: Financial records, email archives, case management data
impact.public_impact: Service disruptions across tax payments, social assistance processing

recovery_steps: Isolate infected segments from network
Activate incident response team and notify CISO
Assess scope: identify encrypted systems via EDR telemetry
Engage forensics: preserve evidence, determine entry vector
Activate DR plan: failover ERP to Pilot Light (AWS)
Restore email from M365 cloud backups
Re-image affected workstations from gold images
Validate Active Directory integrity, reset compromised credentials
Conduct post-mortem and update IR playbook
Report to Ontario IPC if personal data affected

name: Cloud Region Outage - Azure East Canada
description: Microsoft Azure Canada East region experiences a multi-hour outage affecting Azure AD, Azure SQL, and hosted VMs. Public Health records and identity services are unavailable.

impact.public_impact: Immunization clinics disrupted, staff unable to authenticate

recovery_steps: Confirm Azure status via Service Health dashboard
Activate emergency communication (phone tree, SMS)
Switch to backup authentication (on-prem AD FS if configured)
Activate Public Health warm standby in secondary region
Communicate estimated restoration to department heads
Monitor Azure status for region recovery
Validate data consistency post-recovery
Document timeline and lessons learned

name: OT Compromise - Water Treatment SCADA
description: A threat actor gains access to the water treatment SCADA network via a compromised vendor VPN. They attempt to modify the chemical dosing parameters for the water treatment process.

impact.public_impact: Potential public health emergency — unsafe drinking water

recovery_steps: IMMEDIATELY switch to manual override for chemical dosing
Isolate SCADA network from corporate IT network
Revoke all vendor VPN credentials
Notify plant operators to verify water quality manually
Activate incident response team — engage OT security specialist
Forensic imaging of compromised HMI workstations
Restore SCADA from known-good configuration backup
Implement network segmentation improvements (Purdue Model)
Notify Ontario MOE and public health authorities
Conduct joint IT/OT post-mortem

name: Insider Threat - Data Exfiltration
description: A departing employee in Social Services downloads large volumes of personal client data to a USB drive and personal cloud storage. DLP alerts trigger after 48 hours.

impact.data_at_risk: Personal information of Ontario Works clients (MFIPPA protected)
impact.public_impact: Privacy breach affecting vulnerable population, mandatory IPC notification

recovery_steps: Immediately disable employee account and VPN access
Secure and image the employee's workstation
Review DLP logs: identify all files accessed and transferred
Engage HR and Legal — coordinate investigation
Assess scope: how many client records were exfiltrated
Notify Ontario Information and Privacy Commissioner (IPC)
Prepare breach notification for affected individuals
Implement enhanced DLP policies: block USB, restrict cloud uploads
Mandatory offboarding security checklist review
Conduct lessons learned with HR and department management

name: Hardware Failure - Primary Data Center
description: A UPS failure in the primary data center causes an unplanned power outage. On-premises servers including the ERP, traffic management, and GIS systems go offline.

impact.public_impact: Traffic signal disruption at major intersections, delayed financial processing

recovery_steps: Assess data center power status and UPS damage
Activate generator power for critical systems
Failover ERP to AWS Pilot Light environment
Traffic management: dispatch crews to critical intersections for manual control
Boot secondary servers in order of priority tier
Validate database integrity (run consistency checks)
Restore GIS from most recent backup if needed
Engage facilities team for UPS repair/replacement
Update DR plan with lessons on power redundancy
